explanation: The natural language requirement states that the system shall measure and display the tidal volume (the volume of gas delivered to the patient per breathing cycle). Given the available atomic propositions, 'measureTV' refers to the system measuring tidal volume (TV) and 'displayTV' to displaying it. The requirement suggests these should both happen every breathing cycle, but more fundamentally, both must always hold (i.e., whenever their condition arises). Thus, the strongest LTL representation is that it is always the case (globally) that the system measures and displays TV. This can be captured as 'G (measureTV & displayTV)', which means 'always, both measurement and display of tidal volume occurs'.

The requirement specifies that the system shall measure and display the volume of gas delivered to the patient for each breathing cycle (tidal volume). This behavior is best expressed by the property: for every breathing cycle start, the system must ensure that both measurement (measureTV) and display (displayTV) of the tidal volume occur. In LTL, this can be captured using a 'globally' (G) operator: at all times, when a breathing cycle starts (breathingCycleStart), it implies that measureTV and displayTV both become true. We can represent this requirement by using template 17, which states: 'Globally, if bool_exp1, then bool_exp3', where bool_exp1 is breathingCycleStart and bool_exp3 is the conjunction of measureTV and displayTV.

The requirement specifies that in each breathing cycle, the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (the volume of gas delivered per breath). Among the atomic propositions, 'measureTV' corresponds to measurement and 'displayTV' to display of tidal volume. The temporal relationship is that for every breathing cycle (indicated by 'breathingCycleStart'), the system shall ensure that both measurement and display occur. The most fitting template is a global property that, on every occurrence of 'breathingCycleStart', requires both 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' to be true. This is captured by template ID 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 is 'breathingCycleStart' and bool_exp3 is '(measureTV & displayTV)'. This property ensures that whenever a breathing cycle starts, tidal volume must be measured and displayed, directly supporting the stated requirement.

The requirement is that the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (volume of gas delivered per breath cycle). Among the given atomic propositions, 'measureTV' captures the measurement of tidal volume, and 'displayTV' captures displaying it. To ensure the property holds for all breathing cycles, the globally (G) operator is most suitable, expressing that always, both measurement and display of tidal volume occur. Thus, we use the expression G (measureTV & displayTV). The corresponding template is 'G bool_exp3', with bool_exp3 set to 'measureTV & displayTV'.

The requirement states that for each breathing cycle, the system should both measure and display the tidal volume (volume of gas delivered to the patient). There are atomic propositions for both measurement (measureTV) and display (displayTV) of tidal volume. The expression (measureTV & displayTV) captures the condition where, in any breathing cycle, both measurement and display are performed. The natural language implies this must always be true (i.e., in all breathing cycles), so we use the LTL globally operator. Thus, the formula G (measureTV & displayTV) encodes that in all states, the system must be measuring and displaying the tidal volume per breathing cycle.

The natural language requirement is that the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (the volume of gas delivered to the patient) for each breathing cycle. There are atomic propositions for both 'measureTV' (measurement of tidal volume) and 'displayTV' (displaying tidal volume). The property we want to express is that, always, whenever a breathing cycle begins, the system should measure and display the tidal volume. Thus, we choose a template that captures the necessity of these actions per breathing cycle. We use: G(breathingCycleStart -> (measureTV & displayTV)), which corresponds to template 30 ('G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)'), where bool_exp1 is 'breathingCycleStart' and bool_exp3 is '(measureTV & displayTV)'. This expresses that at the start of each breathing cycle, tidal volume is both measured and displayed, satisfying the functional requirement.

The natural language requirement asks for the system to measure and display the tidal volume (volume of gas delivered to the patient per breathing cycle). Among the atomic propositions, there are 'measureTV' for measurement and 'displayTV' for displaying tidal volume. The requirement is best captured with an invariant that whenever the system measures tidal volume in a cycle, it must also display the tidal volume. This is a classic case for the 'Globally, if measured, then must display (possibly immediately)', meaning: G(measureTV -> displayTV). Therefore, template 30 is most appropriate, with bool_exp1 = measureTV and bool_exp3 = displayTV. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement specifies that for every breathing cycle (indicated by 'breathingCycleStart'), the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (TV). The best mapping is to say that after each breathing cycle starts, 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' should occur (either simultaneously or in some order). Since these two can be considered requirements for every breathing cycle, we encode this as: 'Globally, if breathingCycleStart, then (measureTV and displayTV)'. Thus, bool_exp1 is 'breathingCycleStart', bool_exp3 is '(measureTV & displayTV)', and this fits LTL template ID 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3). N_DURATION is irrelevant here as this template doesn't use it.

The requirement states that the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (the volume of gas delivered to the patient per breath). The atomic propositions relevant to this are 'measureTV' (measurement of tidal volume) and 'displayTV' (displaying tidal volume). To fully meet the requirement, both must occur, and this should always be the case: whenever a tidal volume is measured, it must also be displayed. This is best modeled as 'Globally, if tidal volume is measured, then it is also displayed'. This is a safety/liveness property, best captured by template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 encodes measurement and bool_exp3 encodes display. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement states that the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (the volume of gas delivered to the patient per breathing cycle). The atomic propositions 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' directly map to indicating measurement and display of tidal volume, respectively. To fully satisfy the requirement, both must occur for each breathing cycle. To express that both measurement and display always happen together, the Boolean expression (measureTV & displayTV) is used, and the template G bool_exp3 is most suitable to ensure this is always the case throughout system operation.
